title: Motor behavioral evaluation of mice
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"aj24g","text":"This is a general protocol to test motor coordination of mice used models of neurological disorder","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: On first day, mice are acclimated to standing on a non-rotating rod (Rotamex 5, Columbus Instrument) for three 00:01:00 trials with 00:10:00 interval.
Three hours later, mice are acclimated to walking on the rod over three 00:01:30 trials at a speed of 4 rpm
On next day, latency of mice staying in the rotarod is recorded with accelerating speed of 4-40 rpm increasing by 1 rpm every 8.3 seconds for a maximum of 00:05:00 per trial.
Repeat experiment for three trials daily for two days
For actual experiment, mice crosses both beams twice per trial for three trials win 00:05:00 resting periods in between trials
Mice are trained to cross beams of 1~1.5m length of round beam. 24mm and 18mm diameter of beams are used.
Beam-crossing training is done for three times for two days
During the experimental day, its motor coordination will be measured by determining the number of slips, the latency required to cross the beam as the mouse traverses the beam.
creator: Tae-Un Han
